critic_raw: Issue 1: Self-selected adoption. Issue 2: Graduation-season overlap February-May. Issue 3: ATS integration tier confounded with employer sophistication. Issue 4: Staggered regional enablement with aggregate reporting.
defender_raw: Concede all four issues. The pilot is observational and the 34% lift is an unadjusted signal from a self-selected group during a favorable season. A randomized or matched study is required. The feature may still deliver value — this calls for a better-designed test, not rejection.
adjudication_raw: All 4 issues conceded. Both sides recommend a matched or randomized test. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: employer self-selection in opt-in activation
Issue 2: graduation-season overlap in February-May window
Issue 3: ATS integration tier confounded with employer sophistication
Issue 4: staggered regional enablement with aggregate reporting

empirical_test.condition: Randomized encouragement or staged enablement test, or matched difference-in-differences within same recruiting periods, regions, and integration tiers
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: After balancing employer mix, season, region, and integration tier, interview-to-application improvement near zero or inconsistent
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Within matched employers in same recruiting windows and activation tiers, randomly enabled accounts show durable lift of at least 10%
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Feature helps a subset of integrated enterprise employers but most of the aggregate gap explained by mix effects
